task_completion.description: The grainy image is opened in the image editing software (GIMP).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is open, displaying the target grainy image on the canvas.
The 'Filters' dropdown menu at the top of the application window is clicked and expanded.
The 'Enhance' option within the Filters menu is highlighted, showing its submenu.

task_completion.description: The Despeckle filter dialog box is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box titled 'Despeckle' is visible on the screen, showing filter settings like radius or black/white levels.

task_completion.description: The noise reduction is applied, resulting in a less grainy image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final image on the canvas appears noticeably smoother with reduced speckles/grain compared to the original, while the main edges and structural details remain intact (not completely blurred).

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to apply the filter.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with GIMP's menus and dialog boxes, rather than terminal windows running ImageMagick or Python scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GIMP application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application interface matches GIMP, with its standard toolboxes, layers panel, and menu structures visible.

integrity.description: The agent modified the image within the editor rather than downloading a pre-cleaned image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression of screenshots shows the original grainy image being actively modified through the Despeckle dialog preview and final application, rather than a sudden file replacement.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using GIMP. The Despeckle filter is located at Filters -> Enhance -> Despeckle. A successful application should show a visible reduction in grain/noise (at least 15% reduction in smooth regions) while preserving at least 85% of edge details (the image should not look overly blurred or lose its structural integrity).
